description_long: This is a long, rectangular open-frame shelving unit constructed from a grid of horizontal and vertical bars, likely made of metal. The structure features multiple evenly spaced compartments, each formed by the intersecting bars, providing ample storage or display space. The open design allows visibility and access from all sides, making it suitable for urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or garages. The base is solid and flat, supporting the entire frame, while the sides and top are composed of a lattice of bars. There are no visible doors, handles, or enclosed sections, emphasizing its function as an open shelf or rack. The overall appearance is industrial and utilitarian, with a focus on durability and functionality.
description: A long, open-frame metal shelving unit with multiple grid-like compartments and a solid base, designed for urban storage or display. Its industrial, utilitarian design features no doors or handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length and grid structure of the shelving unit with all compartments visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow depth and vertical grid pattern of the shelving unit.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the open grid compartments and solid base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the vertical bars and narrow profile.
